refinement_sections.content: Define the specific characteristics of the customer records dataset, including the size of the dataset, the types of data (e.g., names, emails, phone numbers), and any constraints on the format of this data.
Establish criteria for efficiency in sorting (e.g., time complexity, space complexity) and define what constitutes 'efficient' in the context of this problem.
Clarify the frequency and nature of searches by name, email, and phone number, including whether searches will be conducted simultaneously or sequentially and how quickly results need to be returned.
Identify the maximum memory usage allowed for the sorting algorithm, and specify how this will be measured.
Define the expected volume of concurrent updates to the dataset, including the number of users or processes that will be interacting with the dataset at the same time.
Research and outline potential sorting algorithms that could meet these criteria, considering trade-offs between speed, memory usage, and ease of implementation.
Develop a plan for testing the chosen sorting algorithm, including metrics for performance evaluation and methods for simulating concurrent updates.
